title: thalictrum foetidum var. alpestre
intro: Thalictrum foetidum var. alpestre, also known as Meadow Rue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its distinctive umbrella-like clusters of small white to purple flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Thalictrum foetidum var. alpestre can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to alleviate various ailments.
